summary: The functional test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because pip could not download a package from GitHub due to repeated HTTP 502 errors. The test was retried 3 times (via pytest-rerunfailures) and failed each time with the same network error.
root_cause: GitHub returned repeated 502 responses when pip attempted to download 'https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip', causing the test to fail after exhausting all retries.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: This is the direct error message showing the network failure: GitHub returned too many 502 error responses, causing the pip install to fail.
The test was retried 3 times by pytest-rerunfailures, indicating a transient/flaky network condition rather than a code bug.
The test ultimately failed after all retries were exhausted, confirming the network issue persisted throughout.

evidence.quote: 1 failed, 1080 passed, 44 skipped, 21 xfailed, 2 xpassed, 5 warnings, 3 rerun in 451.05s (0:07:31)
evidence.reason: Only 1 test failed out of 1080 passed, consistent with a transient network failure rather than a code or configuration problem.

suggested_fix: This is a transient network failure caused by GitHub returning 502 errors. No code change is needed; re-running the CI job should resolve the issue. Alternatively, consider marking this test to be skipped or retried more times when GitHub is unreachable.
